title: thymus longicaulis var. subisophyllus
side_effects: Thymus longicaulis var. subisophyllus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high essential oil content. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may also lead to interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially causing adverse effects like bleeding or low blood sugar. In some cases, the plant's essential oils can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes, especially when applied topically without proper dilution or preparation. Additionally, individuals with allergies or sensitivities to certain herbs or essential oils may experience severe reactions, including anaphylaxis, if they use Thymus longicaulis var. subisophyllus improperly.
